channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What to know about Ukraine's military shakeup
item.description: After a week of nationwide protests over the direction of Ukraine's military strategy in the ongoing war with Russia, the Ukrainian military has a new commander, Mykhailo Drapatyi. Here's what to know.
item.content:encoded:
After a week of nationwide protests over the direction of Ukraine's military strategy in the ongoing war with Russia, the Ukrainian military has a new commander, Mykhailo Drapatyi. Here's what to know.

(Image credit: Paula Bronstein for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: A strange transmissible cancer is spreading through the catfish in this lake
item.description: For more than a decade, there's been something fishy going on in a lake that straddles Vermont and the province of Quebec. It involves cancer, a kind of catfish, and — possibly — clues about how tumors metastasize.
item.content:encoded:
For more than a decade, there's been something fishy going on in a lake that straddles Vermont and the province of Quebec. It involves cancer, a kind of catfish, and — possibly — clues about how tumors metastasize.

(Image credit: Joshua Brown)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: In pursuit of 'Instagram face,' are we losing the imperfections that make us human?
item.description: Plastic surgery is becoming so normalized and undetectable, it's changing our relationship to reality. The New Yorker staff writer Jia Tolentino considers how beauty standards have dovetailed with AI.
item.content:encoded: Plastic surgery is becoming so normalized and undetectable, it's changing our relationship to reality. The New Yorker staff writer Jia Tolentino considers how beauty standards have dovetailed with AI.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: People are watching the Reflecting Pool like reality TV. What does that say about us?
item.description: It's not just watching paint dry. The twists and turns of the Reflecting Pool repairs, originally a two-week project, have kept bloggers and viewers busy all summer.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just watching paint dry. The twists and turns of the Reflecting Pool repairs, originally a two-week project, have kept bloggers and viewers busy all summer.

(Image credit: Jackie Lay)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Independent autopsy 'inconclusive' on cause of death for 18-year-old Nolan Wells in Mississippi
item.description: An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells shows that the cause of death is undetermined pending an investigation, according to attorney Ben Crump.
item.content:encoded:
An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells shows that the cause of death is undetermined pending an investigation, according to attorney Ben Crump.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Greetings from Jerusalem, whose holy sites host some of the oldest colonies of nesting swifts
item.description: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.content:encoded: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Trump to attend dignified transfer of fallen soldiers. And, Hegseth testifies on Iran
item.description: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Her son was killed by ICE at a traffic stop. She says she's still waiting for justice
item.description: Ruben Ray Martinez, 23, was fatally shot by an ICE agent two months into President Trump's second term. His mother says her family has yet to receive justice.
item.content:encoded:
Ruben Ray Martinez, 23, was fatally shot by an ICE agent two months into President Trump's second term. His mother says her family has yet to receive justice.

(Image credit: Brenda Bazán for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: U.S. and Iranian attacks continue as tensions mount on a key waterway in the Red Sea
item.description: The fighting comes as the Trump administration is seeking billions of dollars more from Congress for the war with Iran, which Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says has already cost $37.5 billion.
item.content:encoded:
The fighting comes as the Trump administration is seeking billions of dollars more from Congress for the war with Iran, which Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says has already cost $37.5 billion.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: What forecasters expect this hurricane season, as Tropical Storm Bertha edges along
item.description: The El Niño weather pattern could limit how many Atlantic storms form this year. But unusually warm waters temperatures mean the risk of destructive storms is still high.
item.content:encoded:
The El Niño weather pattern could limit how many Atlantic storms form this year. But unusually warm waters temperatures mean the risk of destructive storms is still high.

(Image credit: NOAA via AP)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger
